scenario: Helios SaaS uses Camunda 8.8 to orchestrate customer onboarding through an ad-hoc sub-process that handles 200 customers per hour. A job worker drives the sub-process by activating inner elements through the adHocSubProcess job result and the team needs to know the rule that constrains what a single job result may express.
question: Which constraint applies when the job worker completes its job with the adHocSubProcess job result?

options.text: The job worker can both fulfill the completionCondition and activate new inner elements in a single adHocSubProcess job result response
The job worker may activate new inner elements only when the previously activated elements have all reached a terminated state
The job worker cannot fulfill the completionCondition and activate new inner elements at the same time in one adHocSubProcess job result
The job worker must wait until every inner element has completed before submitting any adHocSubProcess job result back to Zeebe

a.text: Incorrect. The documentation states explicitly that a job worker cannot both fulfill the completion condition and activate new elements in the same job result; the two outcomes must be expressed in separate jobs.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no rule requiring previously activated elements to be terminated before the worker activates additional ones; the worker freely chooses what to activate on each job.

c.text: Correct. Per the job worker implementation, the worker may either activate more inner elements or fulfill the completion condition in a given adHocSubProcess job result, but it cannot do both at the same time.

d.text: Incorrect. Zeebe creates a new job whenever any inner flow completes, so the worker submits job results repeatedly throughout execution rather than waiting for every inner element to finish first.

explanation: On each adHocSubProcess job result, the worker either activates more inner elements or fulfills the completionCondition; the documentation states these two outcomes cannot be combined in a single response, so they happen across separate jobs created by Zeebe.
